summary: In most healthcare facilities like military or large general hospitals, psychiatrists are readily accessible due to higher patient volumes needing their specific care; however, primary health centers lack such specialists owing to resource constraints. Thus, a psychiatrist may not always be present at PHCs but will usually find availability elsewhere as per typical service distribution patterns. Agent2 reasoned that while various types of institutions need psychological services differently based primarily on caseloads and infrastructure capabilities, primary health centers face significant limitations which make consistent presence challenging without compromising other essential functions within those smaller setups. This led them towards concluding option 'A'. The summary captures this rationale succinctly focusing strictly upon critical aspects pertinent directly addressing why certain locations wouldn't regularly house full-time psychiatry coverage
